metadata.title:
TRUCE MADE


WITH |. v. v.

NO MORE STREET SPEAKING
AND NO MORE ARRESTS UN.
TIL TEST IS MACPHERSON.
ERS RELEASED.

pages.raw_text:
Temporary peace was declared
today between the city officials and
the Industrial Workers of the
World. and every one of the so or
more members of the I. w. w., who
have been prisoners in the city Jail
on diet of bread and water for
two days were paroled by Judge
Mann, who had passed sentence
upon them. The Industrial Work
ers of the World agreed that should
this be done no more street meet
ings would be held by any of their
individual members, pending the
decision of the case of Organizer
Walsh, which has been appealed to
the superior court.


The truce was agreed upon in
Mayor Moore's Office this morning
after a conference between the
mayor, Chief of Police Rice, Cor
poration Counsel Hamblen and At
torney Moore, representing the In
dustrfal Workers of the World, on
the understanding that the Walsh
case should be tried out lin the
higher courts to test the validity of
the ordinance against street speak
ing as soon as possible.


Though the city officials had pre-
pared to arrest every violator Of
the ordinance. they are as glad as
the Industrial Workers of the
World that a truce has been effect
ed. AS high as 10 of the prisoners
were crowded into one small cell
and some of them already showed
the effects of their light diet.
